problem_statement: AI-led conversational ('vibe') coding lets developers generate software via natural language, but the AI-generated code raises maintainability, security, and trust concerns that require human oversight before it can be safely adopted in professional practice.
magnitude_evidence: Security scans revealed that vibe coding outputs contained '22% more potential vulnerabilities than manually written code'; also '15% more duplicated logic and 18% higher complexity scores'
insufficiency_evidence: Only 37% of participants felt full confidence in vibe coding outputs without manual review; vibe coding 'should not yet be deployed in mission-critical environments without rigorous oversight'
solution_mechanisms: static analysis and security testing of outputs
context-aware deployment limited to non-critical apps
key_constructs: three-pillar framework (hybrid integration, human oversight, context-aware deployment)
